channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Hong Kong Exchange Proposes Gold Futures as Geopolitical Volatility Drives Demand
item.description: HKEX plans to relaunch gold futures as the city pushes to become an international gold trading hub amid bullion price volatility driven by Iran war tensions.

item.title: Russian Drones Crash in Latvia, Damaging Oil Storage Facility
item.description: Two drones from Russian territory entered NATO member Latvia and crashed, damaging an oil storage facility, in an escalation of spillover from the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: Ukraine Launches Major Drone Attack as Russia Rejects Ceasefire
item.description: Russia says air defenses shot down 347 Ukrainian drones overnight after Moscow rejected Kyiv's ceasefire proposal ahead of Victory Day celebrations.

item.title: Zelenskiy Says Russia Continues to Violate Ukraine Ceasefire
item.description: President Zelenskiy says Russia continues to flout Kyiv-proposed ceasefire, warning Ukraine will continue long-range strikes if Russian assaults persist.

item.title: Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Trump-Vatican Tensions Over Iran
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Pope Leo at the Vatican in a fence-mending visit following Trump's repeated attacks on the pontiff over the Iran war.

item.title: China sentences two former defense ministers to death with reprieve for corruption
item.description: Former defense ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu received suspended death sentences for graft, in the latest high-profile anti-corruption purge targeting China's military leadership.

item.title: UK PM Starmer to seek even closer EU ties after reset deal
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces pressure from MPs to go further than the reset deal as he battles to salvage his premiership, signaling deeper UK-EU integration ahead.

item.title: Turkey Togg partners with China CATL in first overseas EV chassis deal
item.description: Turkish EV maker Togg and Chinese battery giant CATL will jointly develop vehicles based on CATLs Panshi EV platform, marking the first overseas deployment of the Chinese chassis technology.

item.title: China May Attempt Taiwan Maneuvering at Trump-Xi Summit, Taiwan Warns
item.description: Taiwan's intelligence chief warned that China may try to extract concessions on Taiwan when Trump visits Beijing next week, though the U.S. has reaffirmed its policy stance on the island has not changed.

item.title: Vatican Cardinal Calls Trump's Attacks on Pope Leo 'Strange'
item.description: A senior Vatican cardinal criticized Trump's repeated attacks on Pope Leo over the Iran war as "strange," ahead of Secretary of State Rubio's fence-mending visit to the Holy See.

item.title: South Korea and Netherlands Agree to Expand Chips, AI Cooperation
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and Dutch PM Rob Jetten agreed to deepen cooperation on semiconductors, AI, and quantum technology in their first phone call, strengthening ties between two key chip-producing nations.

item.title: China Sentences Former Defense Ministers to Suspended Death for Corruption
item.description: Two former Chinese defense ministers received suspended death sentences on corruption charges, in the latest high-profile anti-corruption purge targeting China's military leadership.

item.title: Moonshot AI valued at $20 billion as Kimi chatbot developer navigates China IPO rules
item.description: The Chinese AI startup behind the popular Kimi chatbot raised $2 billion from Meituan and China Mobile, reaching $3.9 billion in total funding while adapting to Beijings new listing requirements.

item.title: Israel Kills Hezbollah Commander in First Beirut Strike Since Ceasefire
item.description: Israel carried out its first strike on Beirut since the ceasefire, targeting a Hezbollah Radwan Force commander. The attack raises pressure on the fragile truce that had halted Israeli attacks on the Lebanese capital.

item.title: US and Iran Edge Toward Short-Term Deal to End Fighting
item.description: Washington and Tehran are moving toward a memorandum-based agreement rather than a comprehensive peace deal, with Iran reviewing a US proposal. Trump says a deal is "very possible" while threatening continued action if negotiations fail.

item.title: US Fuel Stock Drawdowns Undermine Trump's Oil Price Promises
item.description: Sharp drawdowns in US crude, gasoline and diesel stocks are flashing warning signs, undermining Trump's claims that prices will "drop like a rock" once the Iran conflict ends. Markets remain distorted with reserves at critical levels.

item.title: US-China Consider Launching Official AI Dialogue
item.description: Washington and Beijing are weighing the launch of formal discussions on artificial intelligence, the Wall Street Journal reported, amid broader efforts to stabilize US-China relations ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Israeli Strike Kills Son of Hamas Chief Negotiator in Gaza Talks
item.description: An Israeli airstrike killed the son of Hamas chief negotiator involved in U.S.-mediated talks over Gaza future, potentially complicating ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Shell Reports Strong Q1 Profits as Iran War Drives Oil Trading Gains
item.description: Shell beat profit estimates as the Iran conflict boosted oil prices and generated exceptional trading windfall, highlighting how geopolitical crises benefit major energy traders.

item.title: Asia Faces Plastics Shortage as Middle East War Disrupts Oil Supply
item.description: The Iran conflict has triggered plastics shortages across Asia due to oil and gas supply disruptions, affecting medical supply chains and manufacturing.

item.title: TikTok Invests Record $25 Billion in Thailand Digital Infrastructure
item.description: Chinese firm ByteDance makes massive investment to expand data centers and storage in Thailand, deepening digital infrastructure ties in Southeast Asia.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Wants to Make a Deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that Iran wants to negotiate and make a deal, adding that the US was doing very well in Iran and operations were going smoothly. Tehran is reportedly reviewing a US peace proposal.

item.title: What's at Stake at the Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: President Trump is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping during his visit to Beijing next week, as both countries seek to stabilize a relationship strained by tensions over trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

item.title: Pope Leo to Visit Canary Islands on Spain Trip
item.description: Pope Leo will visit two of the Canary Islands, major entry points for migrants braving dangerous Atlantic waters to reach Europe, as part of a tour of Spain next month.

item.title: Equinor Sees Surge in Asia-Pacific Demand for Norwegian Energy as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Exports
item.description: Norway's state energy giant reports increased interest from customers as far as Australia for petroleum and LNG exports, as Middle East conflict cuts off traditional Gulf supply routes.
